page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core. The processor includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES cryptography. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.g6.large demonstrates entry-level performance characteristics. Its stress-ng single-core CPU performance is categorized as weak, and its multi-core performance falls into the poor category. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only large block speeds of 13.82 GB/sec and write speeds of 11.5 GB/sec, both of which are in the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Similarly, database operations and Redis SET performance, which reaches 1.68 million operations per second, reflect lower-tier throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable, albeit modest, compute resources without the burstable credit limitations of shared-core instances. However, the lack of bundled local storage and low baseline network bandwidth represent notable tradeoffs. This instance is best suited for lightweight workloads such as basic web serving, development environments, small-scale database operations, and general-purpose utility tasks that do not require high memory bandwidth or intensive multi-core processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 2 physical cores with a 35 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 instructions. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results place its CPU and memory performance in the lower tiers, with stress-ng and memory bandwidth tests ranking in the bottom 10% to 25%. Consequently, this server is best suited for lightweight workloads, including basic web serving, development environments, and low-throughput database operations.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core x86_64 virtual machine with balanced memory for entry-level workloads and basic web serving.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 2.5 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
No bundled local storage and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Supports AVX-512 and AES cryptographic instruction sets
Best suited for lightweight web serving, development, and low-throughput databases
